place: Washington Crossing CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Washington Crossing CDP sits on the east bank of the Delaware River, the landing point for George Washington’s 1776 night crossing.
The Washington Crossing Visitor Center Museum houses over 500 artifacts from the American Revolution, donated by the Swan Historical Foundation.
Every weekend, living‑history demonstrations reenact scenes from the Revolutionary War, drawing families and history buffs alike.
The 1904 Washington Crossing Bridge carries Route 29 over the Delaware, linking the New Jersey community to Pennsylvania’s historic park.
Washington Crossing State Park, adjacent to the CDP, preserves the exact spot where Washington’s army crossed the icy Delaware on Christmas night.
Friends of Washington Crossing Park is renovating historic buildings and gardens to celebrate the park’s 250th anniversary in 2026.
